key_insight: Raising the barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% made the detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coating denser and more wear-resistant, though with a slightly higher friction coefficient.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing spray-fill ratio is a practical lever for improving carbide coating integrity and tribological durability in severe-wear industrial components.
method: Detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings at 43% and 53% barrel filling ratios under identical conditions.
Compared microstructure and phase composition using microscopy and XRD.
Evaluated dry sliding wear in ball-on-disc tests at 10 and 15 N and 5 and 10 cm/s.
evidence_signal: The 53% coating achieved the minimum reported wear rate of 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N), consistent with reduced porosity and improved structural integrity.
watch_item: The improved wear resistance came with higher friction, so application choices should balance frictional losses, load conditions, and coating durability.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
